pages.title: A Short Note on Animation History
pages.body: The 1970s shattered the sterile polish of golden-age studio animation. Independent directors like Ralph Bakshi, René Laloux, and Yuri Norstein prioritized texture—xeroxed ink lines, cut-out paper overlays, and raw theatrical vocal tracks. For an actor, voice work ceased to be mere voiceover; it became audio-verité that drove the physical weight of drawn bodies.

title: The Shape of Waiting
direction: Grunge editorial interprets pauses, anticipation and moments just before change, using a page rhythm, type hierarchy and image treatment distinct from recently generated concepts.
description: A personal gift-booklet concept for the Dreamer, built around pauses, anticipation and moments just before change. References should be mixed across several sources so the result feels original rather than like a reskinned template.
spreadNotes: A first spread that establishes the 1990s visual language without becoming a costume copy.
A collage of details, documents and image fragments from licensed or open sources.
A larger composition where the main visual idea crosses the fold.
A restrained final page written directly for the recipient.

title: A Field Guide to Motion
direction: Neo-brutalist publishing interprets speed, balance, direction and the body moving through space, using a page rhythm, type hierarchy and image treatment distinct from recently generated concepts.
description: A personal gift-booklet concept for the Cyclist, built around speed, balance, direction and the body moving through space. References should be mixed across several sources so the result feels original rather than like a reskinned template.
spreadNotes: A first spread that establishes the 2020–2024 visual language without becoming a costume copy.
A collage of details, documents and image fragments from licensed or open sources.
A larger composition where the main visual idea crosses the fold.
A restrained final page written directly for the recipient.

title: After the Last Light
direction: Swiss international editorial interprets the visual transformation that begins when daylight disappears, using a page rhythm, type hierarchy and image treatment distinct from recently generated concepts.
description: A personal gift-booklet concept for the Cinema Lover, built around the visual transformation that begins when daylight disappears. References should be mixed across several sources so the result feels original rather than like a reskinned template.
spreadNotes: A first spread that establishes the 1960s visual language without becoming a costume copy.
A collage of details, documents and image fragments from licensed or open sources.
A larger composition where the main visual idea crosses the fold.
A restrained final page written directly for the recipient.

title: Atlas of Storms
direction: Navigation charts, hard geometry and weather-system diagrams meet warm analogue print colours.
description: A compact visual voyage through storms, routes, lighthouses and the decisions that define a captain. Designed as a personal tribute rather than a general book about the sea.
spreadNotes: A sparse opener built like a navigation document.
Weather symbols become an abstract image system.
A panoramic spread where routes cross a full-width storm field.
A restrained ending around the idea of safe return.

title: Concrete Dreams
direction: Oversized mono typography, raw plans, concrete textures and deliberately severe spacing.
description: A booklet about structures that feel impossible: towers, cantilevers, hidden courtyards and buildings that resemble monuments from another civilisation.
spreadNotes: The title behaves like a physical block.
White space becomes an architectural material.
Plans and photographs overlap without a conventional grid.
A quiet closing spread built around one human-scale detail.

title: Chasing the Light
direction: Wide image fields, dark pauses and small editorial captions that follow changing light.
description: A personal visual essay about the brief moments photographers wait for: fog lifting, a reflection appearing, a street turning gold and night becoming blue.
spreadNotes: A nearly empty page opens the sequence.
A reflected image crosses the fold and breaks the caption grid.
Several small frames create a slow cinematic sequence.
The final page isolates one image and one sentence.

title: Electric Memory
direction: Photocopied textures, compressed typography and abrupt rhythm inspired by independent music publications.
description: A booklet about the sounds, venues, instruments and imperfect recordings that become part of a musician’s identity.
spreadNotes: A torn visual overture with a single loud word.
Venue details and handwritten fragments create atmosphere.
The centre spread resembles an experimental record sleeve.
The last spread removes almost everything except one lyric-like line.

title: Roads Unfinished
direction: Dusty colour, handwritten coordinates and page transitions that resemble changing landscapes.
description: Not a list of destinations, but a book about movement: border crossings, night buses, remote roads and places remembered only by atmosphere.
spreadNotes: The opening resembles a faded travel document.
Objects found along the way become a visual archive.
Urban fragments are arranged like memories rather than reportage.
The ending deliberately leaves the journey unresolved.

title: The Secret Life of Flowers
direction: Historic botanical plates collide with contemporary macro photography and dreamlike cut-paper forms.
description: A gift booklet that sees a garden as a private universe of timing, patience, repetition and unexpectedly strange forms.
spreadNotes: A flower emerges gradually across the opening pages.
Macro detail is treated like an architectural drawing.
Dark botanical forms create a surreal nocturnal chapter.
The final spread shifts from flowers to future possibility.

title: How Giants Are Built
direction: Exploded views, numbered systems and heroic industrial photography arranged with exact visual logic.
description: A tribute to the people who turn drawings into bridges, tunnels, machines and structures larger than any individual.
spreadNotes: A structural diagram introduces the visual grammar.
Small technical differences become the centre of the page.
The centre spread turns construction stages into a single continuous sequence.
The final chapter focuses on the maker rather than the object.

title: Between Worlds
direction: Unreal horizons, translucent forms and typography that appears to drift between foreground and distance.
description: A completely open-ended booklet for someone drawn to dreams, imagined landscapes and things that cannot be explained literally.
spreadNotes: The opener creates a question rather than an answer.
Elements ignore realistic scale and direction.
The centre spread builds an invented world from layered fragments.
The ending dissolves instead of concluding.

title: The Last Train North
direction: Historic timetable structure, winter darkness and bold destination typography with cinematic pacing.
description: A night journey through stations, snow, mechanical detail and the strange emotional pull of a train disappearing into distance.
spreadNotes: A station-board composition establishes time and place.
Signal geometry cuts through a photographic winter field.
The middle chapter becomes intimate and almost silent.
The final page lets the tracks continue beyond the trim.
